info.title: CloudAMQP Instance API
info.description:
This API allows you to manage alarms, integrations, RabbitMQ and LavinMQ, the firewall, VPC peering etc.

[← Back to the CloudAMQP Customer API docs](index.html)

## Authentication

Enter your Instance API key as password. Leave username empty.

**Example using curl:**
```bash
curl -u :xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxx \
  https://api.cloudamqp.com/api/nodes
```

**Getting your Instance API key:**

You can get the Instance API key from the [get instance info endpoint](https://docs.cloudamqp.com/#tag/instances/get/instances/{id}) in the Customer CloudAMQP API. The API key is returned in the `apikey` field.
Or you can find it in the CloudAMQP Console on the Instance details page.

## Terraform Provider

Documentation for CloudAMQP Terraform provider can be found at the [Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudamqp/cloudamqp/latest/docs).

- GitHub Repository: [cloudamqp/terraform-provider-cloudamqp](https://github.com/cloudamqp/terraform-provider-cloudamqp)
- Quick Links
  - [Provider Documentation](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudamqp/cloudamqp/latest/docs)
  - [Instance Resource](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudamqp/cloudamqp/latest/docs/resources/instance)
  - [Alarm Resource](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/cloudamqp/cloudamqp/latest/docs/resources/alarm)

## Additional Resources

- [CloudAMQP Console](https://customer.cloudamqp.com/)
- [CloudAMQP Technical Changelog](changelog.html)

get.description: Shows the current custom domain the cluster is configured for, if any

post.description:
Generates and installs a Let's Encrypt certificate on the cluster as SNI with your custom domain.
This requires that your provided SNI host point towards the RabbitMQ cluster. Otherwise the generation and renewal of the certificate will fail.

**Note:** When creating a custom domain, the listeners on your servers will be restarted in order to apply the changes. This will close your current connections.

hostname.description: The custom hostname. This DNS record must point towards the Rabbit cluster

delete.description: Restores current custom domain configuration. Also stops future renewal process of the certificate.

post.summary: Upload your own certificate to the server.
post.description:
Uploads the certificate to the servers in your cluster.

**Note:** The listeners on your servers will be restarted in order to apply the changes. This will close your current connections.

job_id.description: Job ID to track the certificate upload.

delete.description: Removes the custom certificate from the server and generates a new Let's encrypt cert.

job_id.description: Job ID to track the certificate restore.

get.description: Retrieves current firewall rules

post.description:
Configures the firewall rules. This will reconfigure the firewall - make sure to include all rules in the body, not only the rules you want to add.

Available services: AMQP (5672), AMQPS (5671), HTTPS (443), MQTT (8883), MQTTS (1883), STOMP (61614), STOMPS (61613), STREAM (5552), STREAM_SSL (5551)

**Note:** This action is asynchronous. The request will return immediately, the process to configure the firewall runs in the background. Until completed, consequent requests will return 400 Bad Request.

ip.description: A CIDR for which to open the ports for

services.description: Which services to open up for in the firewall

ports.description: Which ports to open in the firewall

patch.description:
Updates firewall rules. Providing an already existing CIDR will overwrite the current rule. If provided CIDR does not yet exist, it is added upon the other rules. To delete a specific rule, provide the CIDR with empty services and ports.

**Note:** This action is asynchronous. The request will return immediately, the process to configure the firewall runs in the background.

ip.description: A CIDR for which to open the ports for

services.description: Which services to open up for in the firewall

ports.description: Which ports to open in the firewall

put.description: Enable or disable RabbitMQ Firehose tracing (not recommended in production)

enable.description: Whether to enable or disable Firehose

vhost.description: Virtual host to enable tracing on

post.description: Restart the broker on specified nodes or all nodes

nodes.description: Optional list containing a single node name. In a multi-node cluster, only one node can be restarted at a time and all other nodes must be running.

**Note:** This action is asynchronous. The request will return immediately, the process runs in the background.

Status field is always null unless there is an error starting the AWS EventBridge.

get.description:
Get a specific AWS EventBridge integration.
Status field is always null unless there is an error starting the AWS EventBridge.
Retrieve all webhooks

post.description:
Creates a webhook. A POST request will be made for each message in the queue to this endpoint.
If the endpoint returns a HTTP status code in the 200 range the message will be acknowledged and removed from the queue, otherwise retried.

**Signature Verification**: When a `signing_secret` is configured, webhook requests will include headers for signature verification:
- `webhook-id`: Unique message identifier
- `webhook-timestamp`: Unix timestamp (seconds)
- `webhook-signature`: HMAC-SHA256 signature (format: `v1,<base64>`)

The signature is computed over `{webhook-id}.{webhook-timestamp}.{body}`.

**Key Rotation**: Multiple secrets can be specified (space-separated) for zero-downtime key rotation. When multiple secrets are configured, signatures are generated for all of them, allowing consumers to verify with any key during the transition period.

get.description: Retrieve a specific webhook by ID
Retrieve the current RabbitMQ configuration settings

put.description: Update one or more RabbitMQ configuration settings

get.description: Retrieve a list of all RabbitMQ settings that are exposed to changes
Retrieve all log integrations

post.description:
Add an Azure Monitor log integration.

Use Azure portal to configure external access for Azure Monitor. [Tutorial to find/create all params](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/tutorial-logs-ingestion-portal)

table.description: Table name. Must have columns TimeGenerated, Host, Event, Level, Message. [How to create](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/tutorial-logs-ingestion-portal#create-new-table-in-log-analytics-workspace)

dcr_id.description: ID of Data Collection Rule. [Find DCR ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/tutorial-logs-ingestion-portal#collect-information-from-the-dcr)

post.description:
Add a CloudWatch Logs integration.

Need to create an IAM user with programmatic permissions: CreateLogGroup, CreateLogStream, DescribeLogGroups, DescribeLogStreams, PutLogEvents

Optional permissions for retention and tags: PutRetentionPolicy, DeleteRetentionPolicy, TagResource

access_key_id.description: The access key id to use

secret_access_key.description: The secret that goes with the key id

retention.description: (Optional) Number of days to retain log events. [See AWS docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatchLogs/latest/APIReference/API_PutRetentionPolicy.html)

post.description:
Add a Coralogix log integration.

Create a 'Send-Your-Data' private API key. [Coralogix documentation](https://coralogix.com/docs/send-your-data-api-key/)

private_key.description: The API key used for authentication

post.description:
Add a Datadog log integration.

Create a Datadog API key at [app.datadoghq.com](https://app.datadoghq.com)

**Note:** If tags are used, the value part must start with a letter. [See Datadog tagging docs](https://docs.datadoghq.com/getting_started/tagging/#define-tags)

Add a Logentries log integration.

Create a Logentries token at [logentries add-log](https://logentries.com/app#/add-log/manual)

Add a Loggly log integration.

Create a Loggly token at `https://{your-company}.loggly.com/tokens`

Add a Papertrail log integration.

Create a Papertrail endpoint at [papertrail setup](https://papertrailapp.com/systems/setup)

url.description: The URL to push the logs to

post.description:
Add a DataSet (Scalyr) log integration.

Create a Log write token at [DataSet (Scalyr) keys](https://app.scalyr.com/keys)

Add a Splunk log integration.

Create a HTTP Event Collector token at `https://<your-splunk>.cloud.splunk.com/en-US/manager/search/http-eventcollector`

Valid metrics can be found in the [documentation of each integration type](https://www.cloudamqp.com/docs/cloudamqp-integrations.html#metrics).

get.description: List scheduled maintenance operations

post.description: Execute maintenance operation immediately

get.description:
Fetches the current maintenance settings for the cluster.

See [Maintenance by the CloudAMQP team](https://www.cloudamqp.com/docs/cloudamqp-maintenance.html) for more information.

post.description:
Updates the maintenance settings for the cluster.

See [Maintenance by the CloudAMQP team](https://www.cloudamqp.com/docs/cloudamqp-maintenance.html) for more information.

get.description: Retrieve all VPC peering connections

post.description:
Creates a peering request from the VPC hosting your cluster to the VPC you specify.

**Note**: This action is asynchronous. Different parameters are required for AWS vs GCP.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The peering request or connection ID

put.description:
Accepts a pending peering request (AWS only).

**Note**: This action is asynchronous.

get.description: Get PrivateLink/Private Service Connect information

post.description:
Enables PrivateLink (AWS/Azure) or Private Service Connect (GCP).

**Note**: Different parameters required per cloud provider:
- **AWS**: `allowed_principals` - Array of AWS ARN principals. [See AWS PrivateLink documentation](https://www.cloudamqp.com/docs/cloudamqp-vpc-connect.html#aws-privatelink)
- **Azure**: `approved_subscriptions` - Array of Azure subscription IDs. [See Azure PrivateLink documentation](https://www.cloudamqp.com/docs/cloudamqp-vpc-connect.html#azure-privatelink)
- **GCP**: `allowed_projects` - Array of GCP project IDs. [See GCP Private Service Connect documentation](https://www.cloudamqp.com/docs/cloudamqp-vpc-connect.html#gcp-private-service-connect)

allowed_projects.description: GCP project IDs (for GCP Private Service Connect)

put.description: Updates the allowlist for VPC Connect

get.description:
Lists the active VPC endpoint connections to your PrivateLink or Private Service Connect
service. The response fields vary by cloud provider. The `custom_hostname` field is included
if a custom hostname has been configured for the connection, otherwise it is `null`.

200.description: List of active VPC endpoint connections

dns.description: DNS names for the endpoint (AWS only)

custom_hostname.description: Custom hostname configured for this connection, or null if not set

get.description:
Lists all custom hostnames configured for the VPC Connect service. Custom hostnames
provide a DNS record under cloudamqp.com that points to the private IP of the PrivateLink
or Private Service Connect endpoint. The `active` field indicates whether the endpoint
connection still exists. Inactive (orphaned) hostnames can be removed.

active.description: Whether the endpoint connection still exists

post.description:
Sets up a custom hostname for a VPC endpoint connection. The hostname is a DNS record
under cloudamqp.com that matches the instance's TLS certificate, allowing secure
connections through the private endpoint.

- **AWS**: A CNAME record is created pointing to the endpoint's DNS name. No `endpoint_ip` required.
- **Azure/GCP**: An A record is created pointing to the provided private IP address. `endpoint_ip` is required.

endpoint_ip.description: Private IP address of the endpoint (required for Azure/GCP, must be RFC 1918)

delete.description: Removes a previously configured custom hostname for a VPC endpoint connection.

get.description: Retrieve the current OAuth 2.0 configuration for the instance

post.description:
Create a new OAuth 2.0 configuration. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a job_id immediately. Track progress using the Jobs API.

Note: Only one operation can run at a time per resource. When operation is completed, a restart of RabbitMQ is required for changes to be applied.

put.description:
Update the existing OAuth 2.0 configuration. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a job_id immediately. Track progress using the Jobs API.

Note: Only one operation can run at a time per resource. When operation is completed, a restart of RabbitMQ is required for changes to be applied.

delete.description:
Delete the OAuth 2.0 configuration. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a job_id immediately. Track progress using the Jobs API.

Note: Only one operation can run at a time per resource. When operation is completed, a restart of RabbitMQ is required for changes to be applied.

400.description: A pending operation already exists for this resource

get.description: Retrieve the current trust store configuration for the instance

post.description:
Create a new trust store configuration. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a job_id immediately. Track progress using the Jobs API.

The request body varies based on the `provider` field:
- **http**: Fetch certificates from an HTTP/HTTPS URL
- **file**: Upload certificates directly

Note: Only one operation can run at a time per resource.

httpProviderMinimal.description: Provider defaults to 'http' if omitted, refresh_interval defaults to 30 seconds

put.description:
Update the existing trust store configuration. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a job_id immediately. Track progress using the Jobs API.

The request body varies based on the `provider` field:
- **http**: Fetch certificates from an HTTP/HTTPS URL
- **file**: Upload certificates directly

You can switch between providers by including the provider field and the appropriate fields for the new provider type (and setting the old provider's fields to null/omitting them).

Note: Only one operation can run at a time per resource.

422.description: Validation failed or pending job exists

delete.description:
Delete the trust store configuration. This is an asynchronous operation that returns a job_id immediately. Track progress using the Jobs API.

Note: Only one operation can run at a time per resource.

422.description: A pending operation already exists for this resource

get.description: Retrieve the current Job

apiKeyAuth.description:
Enter your Instance API key as password. Leave username empty.

**Example with curl:**
```bash
curl -u :xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxx https://api.cloudamqp.com/api/nodes
```

> [!tip]
> See the [Authentication](#authentication---read-this-first) section in the info block for more details.

type.description: The type of notification to be sent

value.description: Endpoint to where the notification will be sent

value_calculation.description: How to treat value threshold (for disk alarms)

time_threshold.description: Duration in seconds the value_threshold should be active before triggering

value_threshold.description: Value threshold to trigger the alarm (required for cpu, memory, disk)

value_calculation.description: How to treat value threshold (for disk alarms)

time_threshold.description: Duration in seconds (required for cpu, memory, disk)

type.description: Integration type (e.g., papertrail, scalyr, splunk, datadog, etc.)

CustomCert.description: Schema for uploading a custom SSL/TLS certificate, including the key and CA chain.

sni_hosts.description: A hostname (Server Name Indication) that this certificate applies to.

private_key.description: The PEM-encoded private key corresponding to the certificate.

with_headers.description: Include message headers in event data

vhost.description: The VHost the queue resides in

queue.description: A (durable) queue on your RabbitMQ instance

aws_region.description: The AWS region where you want the events to be sent to

aws_account_id.description: The AWS Account ID where you want the events to be sent to

with_headers.description: Include message headers in the event data

signing_secret.description: Secret key(s) for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification. When configured, webhook requests include webhook-id, webhook-timestamp, and webhook-signature headers. Multiple secrets can be specified (space-separated) for key rotation.

webhook_uri.description: The URL to POST the messages to

concurrency.description: Max simultaneous outstanding requests to the endpoint

signing_secret.description: Optional secret key(s) for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification. When configured, webhook requests will include webhook-id, webhook-timestamp, and webhook-signature headers. Multiple secrets can be specified (space-separated) for zero-downtime key rotation.

resource_server_id.description: Unique identifier for the resource server

preferred_username_claims.description: JWT claim names to use for username

additional_scopes_key.description: JWT claim keys containing additional scopes

scope_aliases.description: Map scope aliases to actual scopes (RabbitMQ 4.1.0+)

verify_aud.description: Whether to verify audience claim in tokens

audience.description: OAuth 2.0 audience claim (required for Auth0 with RabbitMQ 4.1+). Typically matches resource_server_id

refresh_interval.description: Interval in seconds to refresh the trust store (default 30)

provider.description: Trust store provider type (defaults to 'http' if omitted)

url.description: URL to fetch certificates from (must be a valid URL with host)

refresh_interval.description: Interval in seconds to refresh the trust store (default 30)

cacertfile.description: Optional CA certificate to verify the HTTPS connection to the trust store URL
CA certificate to verify the HTTPS connection

certificates.description: Array of certificates to upload (1-100 certificates)

name.description: Certificate filename (alphanumeric, dots, hyphens, underscores; max 100 chars; cannot start with '.')

refresh_interval.description: Interval in seconds to refresh the trust store (default 30)

error.description: Error message describing the unknown parameters

unknown_parameters.description: List of parameter names that were not recognized

valid_parameters.description: List of valid parameter names for this endpoint

id.description: Unique identifier for the async operation

account_id.description: ID of the account associated with this operation

resource_type.description: Type of resource being operated on

resource_id.description: ID of the resource being operated on

resource_action.description: Action being performed on the resource

status.description: Current status of the async operation

created_at.description: Timestamp when the operation was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the operation was last updated

error_message.description: Error message if the operation failed, null otherwise

job_id.description: Job ID to track the asynchronous operation

errors.description: Array of validation error objects, each containing field names with their error messages

issuer: must be a valid HTTPS URL
